plan.failureSummary: The test failed during a 'click' action within page.ai because it could not resolve the cached selector for the 'Select your occupation' combobox. The failed selector used a dynamic ID (#\:rl\:-form-item) typical of frameworks like React, which often change between deployments.
observedIndicators: Dynamic ID pattern (#\:rl\:-) detected in failed selector
Semantic intent 'Fill out survey' is highly adaptable to UI changes

remediationSteps.summary: Retry test with cleared page.ai cache.
remediationSteps.details: Execute the test again with the 'clearPageAiCache' directive enabled to force a fresh analysis of the survey page.

remediationSteps.summary: Refine page.ai prompt if dynamic IDs continue to break cache.
remediationSteps.details: If failures persist with specific IDs, consider updating the page.ai prompt to provide more context about the 'Occupation' field, or ensure the application uses more stable data-attributes.

plan.notes: The failure occurred in DETERMINISTIC mode, meaning page.ai was relying on cached instructions. Since the instruction 'Fill out the all of the survey questions' is semantically flexible, clearing the cache and allowing the LLM to re-inspect the page should resolve the selector mismatch.
